what_it_does: Records metadata for a single image attachment, linking an attachment identifier to a target image URI in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only; no body content.
Fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/capital-raise/p-12.
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone.
standouts: Target is an image:// URI under design-source/capital-raise/p-12.
Attachment is typed as 'images' and timestamped.
conventions: Channel-memory attachment stored as a standalone .md file.
Uses image:// URI scheme for asset target.
notes: A timestamped ledger entry that points an attachment record to a specific image asset in the design-source channel.
questions: Is the image:// URI resolvable and versioned?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (attachment metadata record) matches the content.
intention_signals: To create a durable, retrievable record that a specific image belongs to the design-source channel under the capital-raise path.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped pointer that connects an attachment identifier to a specific image asset location, enabling channel-local asset lookup.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file that records an image attachment (att-b339b941) targeting image://design-source/capital-raise/p-12 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image asset attached to the design-source channel for later reference and retrieval.
